before building your next app, answer:

• who is this specifically for?
• when do they open it?
• what disappears after using it?
• how often does that pain show up?
• would they pay $20 today?

if you hesitate on any answer, don’t build it.

I asked for a history of Disneyland. 

It kicked off a deep search, pulled 58 sources, used them to write a script, and generated a 5 minute video with a mix of real and AI visuals.

How it works + a clip

I'm particularly impressed by some of the motion graphics it made to explain concepts. 

For example - how the architects used forced perspective to make the buildings on Main Street look bigger.

The point is that the skills that matter most today in AI-native development are the same ones that matter in traditional product development. Clear communication. Good product judgment. Systems thinking. Great documentation.

This is actually good news if you're a product leader, designer, or engineering manager. The tools are different but the craft is the same. How the work gets done is *totally* different, but your experience isn't obsolete... it's probably more valuable than ever.
